MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas. Thomas Velladao in Propria Persona.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of payment of interest/accrued interest on Mr. Velladao's attorney fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice To Appear On Reconsideration Of Fee Waiver
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Aproving Modification Of Trust Terms Under Probate Code Section 15409(A) (Probate Codes §15409(A) And §17200 (B) (13) )
Off Calendar Jun-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Thomas Lucas (Trustee Ad Litem). Loren Lopin for Martha Gruber.

The Court informed Counsel that ordinarily these matters should be separate petitions and that Ms. Gruber's court fees need to be paid. Mr. Schultz explained that this was a very difficult case in terms of getting everyone to agree, that Mr. Suen was not involved in the settlement because he was a Former Trustee and not a Beneficiary, and that payment of attorney's fees was agreed to but that they did not agree to pay interest. Mr. Lopin stated that he did not participate in the settlement agreement. Mr. Schultz stated that he believes there will be a petition for distribution of the Trust. The Petition for Order Approving Modification of Trust Terms, etc. and Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-10-2012 Continued To Jul-13-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciarin O'Sullivan for Martha Gruber as Trustee. Lorin Lopin for Martha Gruber as Beneficiary. Thomas Velladao appeared by telephone for Ming Suen (Co-Trustee).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to validity of the Nicaraguan divorce (marital status), whether the assets are community property or seperate property, whether or not to go forward with the evidentiary hearing which is scheduled for Friday July 13, 2012, whether the assets are in the Trust and whether service of the Hegstad petition was proper. The Petition for Instructions was continued to July 13, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard with the Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets and the Petition for Order Determining Questions of Construction of Trust Instrument.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

MINI MINUTES: Thomas Belladao for Ming Suen. Ciaran O'Sullivan for Martha Gruber (Co-Trustee). Loren Lopin for Martha Gruber (Beneficiary).

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that the property has not been transferred and expressed reluctance to using the death certificate as it reads with regard to the marital status for recording of the Affidavit of Trustee. Mr. Belladao stated that the divorce Attorney in Nicaragua confirmed that Martha and Ludwig were divorced, and that he hopes to have the divorce document this week as well as a document from Rosemary (Sister) indicating that she has no interest in the Trust property other than those items that were designated to her. Status on transfer of the property was continued to June 26, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an update on turn over of the property and new information regarding the divorce document. Counsel may appear by telephone.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar Apr-24-2012 Continued To Jul-13-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Belladao for Ming Suen. Marilyn Gates for and with Rene Castaneda (Beneficiary). Loren Lopin for Martha Gruber (Beneficiary). Ciarin O'Sullivan for Martha Gruber (Co-Trustee).

The Court expressed concern about the matter, the various petitions that have been filed and raised the issue of an independent Fiduciary being appointed. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to which assets belong to the Trust. The Parties were directed to go out into the hallway and discuss/decide how they are going to proceed. Upon returning, the Parties reported that they did not come to an agreement. A status hearing on transfer of the house was set for May 22, 2012 at 10:30 a.m. If transfer is accomplished by that date there is no need for an appearance. The Parties are also to file statements as to which assets are still in dispute by May 22, 2012. The Petition for Instructions filed by Martha Gruber on calendar for July 2, 2012 was continued to July 10, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. All three petitions (Petition for Order Determing Questions of Construction of Trust/Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets/Petition for Instructions) were set for an evidentiary hearing on any remaining issues for July 13, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a one morning session.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.

Petition For Order Determing Questions Of Construction Of Trust Instrument, Validity Of Trust Provis
Off Calendar Apr-24-2012 Continued To Jul-13-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jan-11-2012 Continued To Apr-24-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciarin O'Sullivan for Martha Gruber (Co-Trustee). Thomas Velladao for Ming Suen.

Mr. O'Sullivan requested a 60-day continuance to obtain more information. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions and provided history. The Court expressed concern about the Co-Trustees not being able to work together, questioned whether an independant Person would be needed, encouraged Counsel to meet within the next week to determine what they can or cannot agree upon, to cooperate with one another and asked them to determine what direction they need to go in. The Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets and the Petition for Order Determining Questions of Construction of Trust Instrument were continued to April 24, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.
